item.title: Version 2.7.1
item.description:
New in 2.7.1

  • Fixes the app failing to open on macOS 15 through 25 (a packaging issue with the bundled media engine). It now runs correctly on every supported macOS version.

item.title: Version 2.7.0
item.description:
New in 2.7.0

  • A fresh new app icon.
  • Built for accessibility: full VoiceOver labels, plus Reduce Motion, Increase Contrast, and Reduce Transparency support.
  • Optional trackpad haptics.

item.title: Version 2.0.3
item.description:
• Cursor now hides automatically with the controls in fullscreen
  • Fixed playback no longer auto-advancing into unrelated files after a single-file open

item.title: Version 2.0.2
item.description:
• Fixed launch crash caused by missing code signing entitlements
  • Fixed window not returning to default size after stopping playback
  • Improved default window sizing for welcome screen

item.title: Version 2.0.1
item.description:
• Smooth, consistent playback on 4K HEVC and Dolby Vision content
  • Near-instant seeking with the slider and scroll wheel
  • Frame-accurate seeking with keyboard shortcuts and bookmarks
  • Reduced background processing during video playback
  • Improved handling of files with many subtitle tracks

item.title: Version 2.0.4
item.description:
• Fixed app hanging indefinitely when opening audio files (especially with embedded album art) from Finder
  • Improved overall responsiveness during file load and music-mode transitions
  • Smoother fullscreen transitions — no more black frame on exit
  • Larger Play/Pause button hit area in the controls bar

item.title: Version 2.0.5
item.description:
• Opening multiple files (or a folder) from Finder now loads them all as a playlist in one window instead of opening separate windows
  • Quick Look extensions (Preview and Thumbnail) now show the Corvus icon and readable names in System Settings
  • Thumbnail generation now falls back to a folder-level cover image (cover.jpg / folder.jpg / album.jpg / front.jpg) when an MKV / WebM / FLAC / WMV file has no embedded artwork

item.title: Version 2.0.6
item.description:
• Re-ships the multi-file Open With fix that should have been in 2.0.5 — opening a whole folder or selection from Finder now correctly loads everything as a single playlist instead of spawning a window per file
  • Quick Look extensions now cover every audio and video format Corvus Player can play: WAV, WMA, OGG, Opus, DSF, DFF and more
  • Embedded album-art thumbnails now work for Opus, OGG and FLAC files (Vorbis Comment METADATA_BLOCK_PICTURE), in addition to MP3 and M4A
  • Folder-cover thumbnail fallback (cover.jpg / folder.jpg / album.jpg / front.jpg) for audio files without embedded artwork

item.title: Version 2.1.0
item.description:
Album art and video previews in Finder, for every supported file

  • New: songs and videos you save now get real album art (audio) or a representative video frame (video) as their Finder icon, automatically. Works for every format Corvus Player supports, including the ones macOS normally shows as a generic music note or film strip (opus, ogg, mkv, webm).
  • New: a background helper updates Finder thumbnails the moment you save a new file, even when Corvus Player is fully closed. Toggle it in Settings › Advanced › Permissions.
  • New: first-launch walkthrough explains the macOS switches Corvus Player can use, in plain English. Reachable any time from Settings › Advanced › Permissions.
  • New: File › Generate Finder Thumbnails… lets you bulk-process any folder on demand.
  • Improved: Recent files grid now shows the actual album cover for music tracks and a proper video frame for video, with consistent tile alignment.
  • Improved: opening multiple files at once via Open With no longer briefly flashes a second window before the playlist loads.
  • Improved: Settings search now finds the new Permissions section, and the search field finally drops focus when you click outside it.

item.title: Version 2.1.1
item.description: • Fixed the player window briefly flickering on and off when opening several files at once from Finder

item.title: Version 2.2.0
item.description:
A real audio visualizer for Music Mode

  • The Music Mode visualizer now shows a true frequency spectrum, so the bars move with the actual bass, mids, and treble in your music instead of just the overall volume. No extra permissions needed.
  • Four visualizer styles: Spectrum Bars, Mirrored, Waveform, and Spectrum Curve, plus an option to color the bars using your album artwork.
  • Album Blur is now the default Music Mode background, with your cover's colors softly blurred behind the artwork.
  • Smoother, more responsive bars that stay in sync with playback.

Fixes

  • Fixed a crash that could occur while Corvus Player generated album art and video thumbnails in the background.

item.title: Version 2.3.0
item.description:
Synced lyrics in Music Mode

  • New: karaoke-style, time-synced lyrics in Music Mode. Lyrics are fetched automatically from LRClib or loaded from a local .lrc file next to your track, with the current line highlighted and click-to-jump on any line.
  • New: an optional audio visualizer can sit below the album art while lyrics are showing. Toggle it in Settings.
  • New: a refreshed Corvus Player app icon.

Improvements and fixes

  • Play, pause, and skip from earphones and media keys now work reliably; Corvus Player no longer loses the system Now Playing controls.
  • The playlist now scrolls to keep the playing track in view, with a smooth glide and room to see what plays next.
  • Selecting a track from the playlist now starts it from the beginning instead of resuming where it last left off.
  • Fixed a loading spinner that could get stuck while scrolling a long playlist.
  • Added a Feedback link to the About page.

item.title: Version 2.4.0
item.description:
More features, now free

  • The audio visualizer, mini player, screenshots, and audio passthrough are now free for everyone. Corvus Pro keeps the power-user extras: the full preset system, custom EQ and HDR presets, custom themes, the advanced visualizer styles, and the command-line tool.

Improvements

  • The Dock right-click menu now puts Play, Pause, and Stop at the bottom, closest to the Dock, so they are quicker to reach.
  • Corvus Player now shows release notes the first time you open it after an update.
  • The Pro upgrade and license screens are cleaner; your license key is no longer shown, with simple Apply and Revoke actions.

item.title: Version 2.4.1
item.description:
Fixes

  • Fixed a freeze that could happen when opening several files at once.

item.title: Version 2.4.2
item.description:
Fixes

  • Fixed the player window flickering when opening several files while it was already open.

item.title: Version 2.5.0
item.description:
New: a dedicated Equalizer

  • A new Equalizer tab with a 10-band equalizer, adjustable per-band width, and saveable presets.
  • Device Equalization: AutoEQ corrections for 6,000+ headphones and earphones, matched to your output device automatically.
  • Sound shaping: crossfeed, loudness compensation, channel balance, and mono.
  • Import, export, and per-device EQ memory, plus an Equalizer menu with keyboard shortcuts.

item.title: Version 2.6.0
item.description:
New in 2.6.0

  • Night mode evens out loud and quiet parts so late-night dialog stays clear.
  • Pinch to zoom and pan into the video, with an Up Next queue to line up what plays next.
  • A menu bar mini player, plus Shortcuts, Spotlight, and Focus filter support.
  • Find subtitles from SubDL as well as OpenSubtitles, with one-tap styling presets.
  • Streams now cache to disk, so jumping back is instant.
